name: Cha-Tel
description_short: Cha-Tel Federal Credit Union is a Charleston, WV-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services to members in its service areas.
description_long:
Cha-Tel Federal Credit Union (Cha-Tel FCU) is a federally chartered credit union serving the Charleston, West Virginia community and surrounding service areas. Founded to enhance financial well-being for its members, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration). The institution maintains a physical branch network alongside comprehensive digital services.

Cha-Tel FCU offers a full suite of consumer and business banking products. On the deposit side, members can access high-yield checking accounts (Share Draft Accounts), regular savings accounts (Share Savings), and certificates of deposit (Share Certificates/CDs). The credit union reported 658 active checking accounts with $1,845,993 in total share drafts, 1,311 active savings accounts, and 153 active CDs as of the content update (April 2026). Lending products include flexible auto loans, mortgage options, and business loans with personalized support.

Cha-Tel distinguishes itself through robust digital banking infrastructure, including mobile banking, bill pay, electronic statements, external account transfers, and mobile payments. The credit union emphasizes member benefits such as exclusive discounts, dedicated financial planning, and wealth management services. Their online loan application process streamlines access to financing. As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, Cha-Tel operates on a not-for-profit cooperative model where members are owners, potentially allowing for competitive rates and member-focused service.

A key limitation is that detailed information about rates, fees, loan terms, and specific eligibility requirements is not provided on the website content reviewed. The profile relies on NCUA data and service descriptions rather than comprehensive rate comparisons or regulatory filings. Potential members should contact the credit union directly at (304) 344-3330 for current rates and specific product details. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft Accounts) with debit card and check access
Savings accounts (Regular Share Savings) with competitive interest rates
Certificates of Deposit (Share Certificates) with low-minimum balances
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinance
Business loans for growth and expansion
Mobile banking app for account management and payments
Online bill pay with one-time and recurring payment scheduling
Electronic statements (e-Statements) and paperless account access
Financial planning and wealth management services
Loan calculator tools for auto, mortgage, and savings planning
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers competitive rates and lower fees than commercial banks
658 active checking accounts with free share drafts and no monthly fees available
Comprehensive mobile and online banking platform with bill pay, transfers, and e-statements
Offers business loans with flexible financing and personalized support for local growth
Low-minimum Share Certificates (CDs) available for higher-yield savings
Electronic signature authentication and mobile payments for secure, convenient transactions
Local focus on Charleston, WV community with dedicated branch access
cons: Website does not display current interest rates for checking, savings, or CDs, requiring direct contact for rate comparison
Limited geographic presence — primarily serves Charleston, WV and defined service areas, not nationwide
No information provided about minimum account balances, membership eligibility requirements, or fee schedules
Lack of details on loan approval timelines, APR ranges, or specific lending terms despite offering auto loans and mortgages
Website content incomplete — several sections cut off mid-sentence, suggesting outdated or improperly maintained information
best_for: Charleston, WV residents seeking a member-owned alternative to commercial banks with local branch access
Small business owners needing flexible, personalized business loans with community-focused service
Savers looking for certificate of deposit (CD) products with competitive rates through a cooperative financial institution
Mobile-first consumers who want comprehensive digital banking including bill pay, transfers, and account management on smartphones
diagnosis: Cha-Tel FCU is best for Charleston, WV residents and businesses seeking traditional credit union banking with digital convenience, competitive member-owned rates, and local community focus. The primary caveat is that the website lacks transparent rate and fee information, requiring direct contact for rate comparisons before account opening or loan application.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
